bold.purpose: Titles, mode pills, selected rows, hint key letters. Carries weight contrast in the absence of size scale.

regular.purpose: Body, captions, meta. Tone (text/textMuted/dim) carries hierarchy at this weight.

motion.name: border-pulse
motion.purpose: Running and awaiting-input node-card borders pulse. Steady-state colors do not pulse.

motion.name: spinner-braille
motion.purpose: The single loading affordance. Color matches the surrounding text role.

breakpoints.name: sidebar-collapse
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar collapses below 80-character terminal width. Layout is column-based, not pixel-based.

components.name: Mode Pill
components.description: Saturated rectangular badge. Telegraphs which mode/phase the user is in (GRAPH, PICK, PROMPT, CONFIRM).
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-pill--mode { background: #89b4fa; color: #313244; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Status Pill (Confirm)
components.description: Variant of the mode pill, used when the picker is in the confirm phase or the orchestrator has completed.
components.css: .ds-pill--confirm { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; line-height: 1.4; background: #cba6f7; color: #313244; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Picker Row (default)
components.description: Unselected workflow row. Mantle background, muted text, leading two-space indent.
components.css: .ds-picker-row { background: #181825; color: #a6adc8; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; padding: 2px 16px 2px 8px; white-space: pre; }

components.name: Picker Row (selected)
components.description: Selected workflow row. Accent background, surface0 foreground, bold name and chevron — the accent-pill vocabulary applied to a list row.
components.css: .ds-picker-row--selected { background: #89b4fa; color: #313244; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; padding: 2px 16px 2px 8px; font-weight: 700; white-space: pre; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: Rounded bordered box. Mantle interior, overlay0 border. The default container for groups of content.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #181825; color: #cdd6f4; border: 1px solid #6c7086; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Field (focused)
components.description: Input/textarea/enum container. Border shifts to primary blue when focused; interior lifts one stratum from crust to mantle.
components.css: .ds-field { display: block; position: relative; padding-top: 10px; } .ds-field__label { position: absolute; top: 4px; left: 12px; padding: 0 4px; background: #1e1e2e; color: #cdd6f4; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 12px; } .ds-field__input { background: #181825; color: #cdd6f4; border: 1px solid #89b4fa; border-radius: 6px; padding: 6px 12px; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; outline: none; width: 280px; } .ds-field__input::placeholder { color: #7f849c; } .ds-field__input:focus-visible { border-color: #89b4fa; }

components.name: Statusline
components.description: Single-row chrome bar pinned to the bottom of the orchestrator panel. Mode pill flush left, hints right-aligned.
components.html: GRAPH↑↓←→ navigate · ↵ attach · q quit
components.css: .ds-statusline { display: flex; align-items: center; background: #313244; color: #cdd6f4; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; height: 24px; padding: 0 16px 0 0; } .ds-statusline__spacer { flex: 1; } .ds-statusline__hints { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; } .ds-statusline__key { color: #cdd6f4; font-weight: 700; } .ds-statusline__label { color: #a6adc8; } .ds-statusline__sep { color: #7f849c; }

components.name: Node Card (running)
components.description: Orchestrator graph node. Background stays on canvas; border carries status. Yellow border pulses while a stage is running.
components.css: .ds-node-card { background: #1e1e2e; border: 1px solid #f9e2af; border-radius: 6px; width: 220px; height: 64px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; animation: ds-pulse 1.6s ease-in-out infinite; } .ds-node-card__title { color: #cdd6f4; font-size: 12px; position: absolute; top: -8px; background: #1e1e2e; padding: 0 6px; } .ds-node-card__duration { color: #f9e2af; font-size: 13px; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { border-color: #585b70; } 50% { border-color: #f9e2af; } }

narrative.overview:
Atomic is a terminal-native control plane for AI coding agents. Engineers run hour-long autonomous sessions through it, then come back to review. The interface has to disappear into that work: glanceable at 2am, dense without being cluttered, and willing to surface a saturated color the moment something needs attention. Catppuccin Mocha is the canonical palette; Tokyo Night is the secondary fallback. Light terminals fall back to Catppuccin Latte through the same token roles, so every component below has a parallel light reading.

The visual register is Restrained. Most of the surface is tinted neutral, with one accent (Catppuccin Blue) reserved for primary actions, current selection, and the mode pill. Status colors are not decoration; they correspond to the orchestrator's own state vocabulary (running, complete, awaiting input, error, pending). Bold weight does the heavy lifting where size cannot.

What Atomic is not: a cluttered enterprise dashboard, a generic CLI dump, or a flashy demo. There is no gratuitous animation. There is no decoration without a state behind it.
keyCharacteristics: Catppuccin Mocha tokens, role-mapped (bg, backgroundPanel, backgroundElement, surface, selection, border, borderDim, accent, text, textMuted, dim, semantic colors)
Restrained color strategy: one accent at a time, status colors carry meaning
Tactile and confident component voice: pills, rounded boxes, decisive selection states
Depth via tonal layering only; no shadows are possible in a terminal
Bold + dim contrast carries hierarchy where size cannot

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Catppuccin Blue is used on ≤10% of the surface at any time — the mode pill, the selected picker row, the focused field's border. Saturating two regions simultaneously breaks the rule: pick which moment carries the accent and let the other go quiet.

rules.name: The Status-Is-Truth Rule
rules.body: Yellow, Green, Red, Sky, and dim-Overlay1 map one-to-one to the orchestrator's session statuses. Never use a status color decoratively. If a node-card border is yellow, an agent is running.

rules.name: The Pulse Rule
rules.body: Running and awaiting-input borders pulse via lerpColor(border, hue, t) where t follows a sine. Steady-state colors don't pulse — pulse means 'this is live, watch it.'

rules.name: The Weight-and-Tone Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is bold/regular × text/textMuted/dim. Six combinations cover the entire system. Inventing a seventh (italics, underline, blinking) is a smell — find the existing slot.

rules.name: The No-Decorative-Bold Rule
rules.body: Bold marks emphasis (titles, pills, hint key letters, focused selection). Never bold an entire line of body copy or a description.

rules.name: The Strata Rule
rules.body: Every new component must declare which stratum it lives on (crust, mantle, base, surface0, or accent-pill). Mixing two strata inside one component is the smell — split it into stacked components instead.

rules.name: The No-Glow Rule
rules.body: Focus states do not glow, blur, or grow. They shift one stratum (field interior crust → mantle) and recolor the border (border → primary). That is the entire focus vocabulary.

rules.name: The Pill-Means-Mode Rule
rules.body: A saturated pill always means 'you are in this mode.' Don't use the pill shape for a static label or a count.

rules.name: The Rounded-Or-Nothing Rule
rules.body: Bordered boxes use borderStyle='rounded'. Square borders, ASCII +---+ art, and double-line borders are out of vocabulary.

dos: Do use Catppuccin Mocha tokens addressed by role (accent, success, warning, info, error, text, textMuted, dim). Never inline a hex code in a component.
Do mirror the accent-pill vocabulary (primary bg + surface0 fg + bold) across every 'you are here' state — mode badges, selected rows, header session pills.
Do use Unicode icons from the established set: ✓ ✗ → ↵ ↑↓←→ ○ ● ◆ ▸ ▎ ❯ │.
Do respect NO_COLOR. Color carries semantics; the layout must still parse without it.
Do keep all interactions reachable via keyboard. Every selection state has a key binding.
Do lean on bold + text/textMuted/dim to carry hierarchy. Six combinations cover the system.
Do pulse running and awaiting-input borders. Steady-state colors don't pulse.
Do write hints as <key> <label> separated by ' · '. Active keys text, labels textMuted.
donts: Don't ship cluttered enterprise dashboards or information overload. Density without clarity is the failure mode.
Don't ship generic or boilerplate CLI output. Atomic should feel crafted.
Don't add gratuitous animation, ASCII art overload, or flashy/gimmicky UI. Function over novelty.
Don't introduce loading states for instantly-renderable content. No spinners on prompt; no flash.
Don't use emoji. Unicode glyphs only.
Don't saturate two regions with the accent simultaneously. The mode pill or the selection — not both.
Don't use a status color decoratively. Yellow means running, sky means awaiting input.
Don't invent a side-stripe border-left accent on rows or cards. Background tints, full borders, leading icons, or nothing.
Don't use square borders or ASCII +---+ art. Rounded only.
Don't introduce italics, underline, blinking, or any text decoration outside bold/regular.
Don't mix two strata inside one component. Split into stacked components.
Don't wrap focus states in glow, blur, or growth. Shift one stratum and recolor the border, that's it.
